figures.caption: Table 1: Performance Comparison on MOSI and MOSEI. ↑ and ↓ indicate that higher or lower value is better. Bold means best.
Table 3: Ablation studies of DBR on MOSI and MOSEI.
Figure 3: t-SNE visualization of feature distributions onMOSI. Warmer colors indicate more positive sentiment. The full DBR variant produces a clearer sentiment gradient.

Figure 4: Attention distributions from the BRF module on theMOSI (top) and CMU-MOSEI benchmark (bottom) test set. Numbers denote attention values. 𝐹𝑠ℎ𝑎 , 𝐹𝐴, 𝐹𝐿 , 𝐹𝑉 are shared, acoustic, linguistic and visual components, respectively.
Figure 5: Analysis of temporal-structural factorization on MOSI.
Figure 7: Sensitivity analysis of DBR on MOSI and MOSEI.

Figure 2: Architecture illustration of DBR. The left shows the multimodal decoupling . The center highlights the TSF branch and the AGPR branch. Only interactions between the (yellow) visual and (blue) acoustic modalities are shown, with analogous cases for other modalities indicated by dotted lines. The right presents the BRF, exemplifying the fusion of the (yellow) visual modality with 𝐹all. Similar fusion processes for other modalities are omitted.
